prompts:
[User]
You synthesizer model fusion panel. Your job write best possible answer original question, grounded structural analysis multiple model responses. ## Original Question one sentence, what CAP theorem? ## Panelist Responses ### claude-opus-4-8 CAP theorem states that distributed data store simultaneously guarantee most two following three properties: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance. --- ### glm-5.2 CAP theorem states that distributed db system only simultaneously provide two following three guarantees: consistency (every read receives most recent write), availability (every request receives non-error response), and partition tolerance (the system continues operate despite network comm failures). --- ### qwopus3.6-27b-v2 CAP theorem states that distributed system only guarantee two out three following properties any given time: Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. ## Judge Analysis (structural extraction) { "consensus": [ { "point": "CAP theorem applies distributed systems/data stores", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "glm-5.2", "qwopus3.6-27b-v2" ] }, { "point": "Only two three properties guaranteed simultaneously", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "glm-5.2", "qwopus3.6-27b-v2" ] }, { "point": three properties Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "glm-5.2", "qwopus3.6-27b-v2" ] } ], "contradictions": [ { "topic": "Scope system type", "positions": [ { "panelist": "claude-opus-4-8", "claim": "Applies distributed data store" }, { "panelist": "glm-5.2", "claim": "Applies distributed db system" }, { "panelist": "qwopus3.6-27b-v2", "claim": "Applies distributed system (more general)" } ] } ], "unique_insights": [ { "panelist": "glm-5.2", "insight": "Provides parenthetical definitions each property: consistency (every read receives most recent write), availability (every request receives non-error response), partition tolerance (continues operate despite network comm failures)" } ], "blind_spots": [ "No panelist mentioned practical implication: during network partition, system must choose between Consistency and Availability", "No panelist noted CAP also known Brewer's theorem", "No panelist gave real-world examples (e.g., CP systems like HBase, AP systems like Cassandra)", "No panelist clarified that Partition Tolerance not optional distributed systems" ], "confidence": [ { "panelist": "claude-opus-4-8", "score": 0.82, "notes": "Correct core concept but grammatical errors ('guarantee most two') reduce clarity; no definitions provided" }, { "panelist": "glm-5.2", "score": 0.95, "notes": "Most complete and clear; provides definitions each property and clean phrasing" }, { "panelist": "qwopus3.6-27b-v2", "score": 0.88, "notes": "Clear and correct statement; uses 'any given time' qualifier; lacks property definitions" } ], "shared_artifacts": [ "All three open with identical boilerplate: 'CAP theorem states that'", "All three list properties same order: Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance" ], "injection_suspected": false } ## Your Task Write single, authoritative answer original question. Follow these rules: 1. **Lead with consensus.** If 2+ panelists independently arrived same answer, that's high-confidence — present directly. 2. 3. 4. **Fill blind spots.** If judge identified aspects nobody covered, address them if you 5. 6.
